skill.name: normalize
skill.description: Align an existing frontend feature with the product design system, component patterns, accessibility expectations, and quality checks.
skill.summary: Normalize frontend UI work to match established design system standards.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found prose-only design instructions, and the matches are false positives caused by design terms such as patterns, tokens, and grid systems. No scripts, network access, environment access, external commands, or audit-bypass prompt injection were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on multiple SKILL.md lines, but the file contains only natural-language design guidance. No hashing, encryption, credential handling, or executable cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows design-system prose only. SKILL.md contains no code, cryptographic API calls, encoded payloads, or credential handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match in Layout Guidance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 49, but the line discusses spacing, layout, grid systems, and layout patterns. No operating system discovery, host enumeration, or command execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is UI layout guidance and contains no executable instruction to inspect host systems. There are no scripts or shell commands elsewhere in the scanned file.

content.user_title: Normalize Interfaces to Your Design System
content.value_statement: Frontend features often drift from shared design standards as teams iterate quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured UI normalization.
actual_capabilities: Finds design system documentation, UI guidelines, component libraries, and style guides before editing.
Compares a feature against established typography, color, spacing, layout, and component patterns.
Creates a normalization plan for replacing one-off UI with design system equivalents.
Checks responsive behavior, motion, interaction patterns, information hierarchy, and accessibility expectations.
Prompts cleanup of orphaned code and verification with repository linting, type checks, and tests.
limitations: Requires existing design system context or a prior teach-impeccable setup.
Does not provide its own tokens, components, or visual assets.
Depends on repository-specific tools to run linting, type checks, and tests.
May need human clarification when design principles are incomplete or conflicting.

use_cases.title: Align a New Feature
use_cases.description: Review a recently built route and bring typography, color, spacing, and components into the design system.

use_cases.title: Reduce UI Drift
use_cases.description: Audit an older screen and replace custom styles with shared tokens, components, and interaction patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Product Review
use_cases.description: Normalize visual and accessibility details before a release review or stakeholder demo.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize One Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize the settings page against our design system. Ask for missing design context before changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Audit UI Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the billing flow for design system drift. Create a short plan, then align components, spacing, and accessibility states.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Custom Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize the onboarding feature by replacing one-off UI with shared components and tokens. Verify responsive behavior and cleanup obsolete code.

prompt_templates.title: System-Wide Consistency Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the admin dashboard to established design system patterns. Prioritize usability, accessibility, and maintainable shared UI before visual polish.

output_examples.input: Normalize a profile settings page that uses custom spacing and button styles.
output_examples.output: A concise plan, updated UI styling, shared component usage, and verification notes for linting and accessibility checks.

output_examples.input: Review a checkout flow that looks different from the rest of the app.
output_examples.output: A drift assessment covering typography, color, spacing, interactions, responsive behavior, and recommended implementation changes.

output_examples.input: Bring a legacy admin table into the current design system.
output_examples.output: A normalized table experience using established components, consistent density, accessible focus states, and cleanup recommendations.

best_practices: Provide the route, feature name, and any relevant design system documentation.
Ask for a plan before implementation when the feature has broad user impact.
Run the repository quality checks after UI changes are complete.
anti_patterns: Using this skill to invent a new design system from scratch.
Skipping clarification when tokens, components, or personas are unclear.
Accepting visual consistency changes that weaken accessibility or usability.

faq.question: What does this skill normalize?
faq.answer: It normalizes frontend features against an existing design system, including components, tokens, layout, motion, responsiveness, and accessibility.

faq.question: Does it require a design system?
faq.answer: Yes. It works best when documentation, components, tokens, or clear examples already exist in the repository.

faq.question: Can it create new components?
faq.answer: It can suggest shared components when needed, but it prefers existing design system equivalents.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to use repository linting, type checks, and tests when those tools are available.

faq.question: Is it only for visual polish?
faq.answer: No. It also focuses on usability, responsive behavior, information hierarchy, interaction patterns, and accessibility.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
